Right atrium.name: Right Atrium
Right atrium.function: Receives deoxygenated blood returning from the body through the superior and inferior vena cava, then pumps it through the tricuspid valve into the right ventricle. Home of the sinoatrial (SA) node — the heart's natural pacemaker.

Right ventricle.name: Right Ventricle
Right ventricle.function: Pumps deoxygenated blood through the pulmonary valve into the pulmonary trunk and on to the lungs to pick up oxygen. A lower-pressure pump than the left ventricle.

Left atrium.name: Left Atrium
Left atrium.function: Receives freshly oxygenated blood from the lungs via the four pulmonary veins and pushes it through the mitral valve into the left ventricle.

Left ventricle.name: Left Ventricle
Left ventricle.function: The strongest chamber — its thick muscular wall drives oxygenated blood through the aortic valve into the aorta and out to the entire body. Generates the systolic blood pressure you can feel as a pulse.

Anterior papillary muscle of right ventr.name: Anterior Papillary Muscle (RV)
Anterior papillary muscle of right ventr.function: A finger of muscle inside the right ventricle. Its chordae tendineae anchor the tricuspid valve leaflets, stopping them flipping backward when the ventricle contracts.

Inferior papillary muscle of right ventr.name: Inferior Papillary Muscle (RV)
Inferior papillary muscle of right ventr.function: Tethers the inferior tricuspid leaflet via the chordae tendineae, keeping the valve sealed during right-ventricular contraction.

Septal papillary muscle of right ventric.name: Septal Papillary Muscle (RV)
Septal papillary muscle of right ventric.function: Small papillary muscle on the septal wall of the right ventricle; supports the septal leaflet of the tricuspid valve.

Inferior papillary muscle of left ventri.name: Inferior Papillary Muscle (LV)
Inferior papillary muscle of left ventri.function: Anchors the mitral valve via chordae tendineae so it withstands the high pressure of left-ventricular contraction without leaking.

Inferior leaflet of right atrioventricul.name: Tricuspid Valve · Inferior Leaflet
Inferior leaflet of right atrioventricul.function: Part of the tricuspid (right AV) valve between the right atrium and right ventricle. Opens to let blood fill the ventricle and snaps shut to prevent backflow during contraction.

Septal leaflet of right atrioventricular.name: Tricuspid Valve · Septal Leaflet
Septal leaflet of right atrioventricular.function: The septal cusp of the tricuspid valve, attached near the interventricular septum. Together the three cusps form a one-way gate into the right ventricle.

Posterior leaflet of left atrioventricul.name: Mitral Valve · Posterior Leaflet
Posterior leaflet of left atrioventricul.function: A cusp of the mitral (bicuspid / left AV) valve between the left atrium and left ventricle. Holds back high-pressure blood during systole so it flows forward into the aorta, not backward.

Right coronary leaflet.name: Aortic Valve · Right Coronary Cusp
Right coronary leaflet.function: One of three half-moon cusps of the aortic valve. The right coronary artery arises just above it. Opens as the left ventricle ejects blood into the aorta, then closes to stop backflow.

Left coronary leaflet.name: Aortic Valve · Left Coronary Cusp
Left coronary leaflet.function: Aortic valve cusp above which the left coronary artery originates, feeding the heart muscle itself. Closes during diastole to keep blood in the aorta.

Non-coronary leaflet.name: Aortic Valve · Non-coronary Cusp
Non-coronary leaflet.function: The third aortic cusp, with no coronary artery above it. The three cusps seal together to hold the entire aortic blood column above the resting ventricle.

Anterior semilunar leaflet of pulmonary.name: Pulmonary Valve · Anterior Cusp
Anterior semilunar leaflet of pulmonary.function: A cusp of the pulmonary valve at the exit of the right ventricle. Opens to send deoxygenated blood toward the lungs and closes to prevent it draining back.

Left semilunar leaflet of pulmonary valv.name: Pulmonary Valve · Left Cusp
Left semilunar leaflet of pulmonary valv.function: Left half-moon cusp of the pulmonary valve guarding the route into the pulmonary trunk and lungs.

Right semilunar leaflet of pulmonary val.name: Pulmonary Valve · Right Cusp
Right semilunar leaflet of pulmonary val.function: Right cusp of the pulmonary valve. With the other two cusps it forms the one-way gate from the right ventricle into the pulmonary circulation.
